relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Surrounded by witnesses, run with endurance, laying aside every weight.
Look to Jesus, pioneer and completer of trust.
Consider his endurance so your souls do not faint.
The struggle has not yet reached blood.
Do not forget the encouragement spoken to children.
Divine discipline is framed as love and training.
Endurance receives training as children.
Shared training marks belonging.
The Father of spirits trains toward life.
Divine training is for benefit, sharing holiness.
Training is painful now but yields peaceful fruit of justice.
Lift drooping hands and weakened knees.
Make straight paths so the lame may be healed.
Pursue peace with all and holiness.
Guard against falling short of grace and bitter roots.
Esau warns against selling inheritance for appetite.
Tears could not recover what was traded away.
You have not come merely to touchable terror.
The voice once made hearers beg for no more.
The mountain command was unbearable.
Even Moses trembled at the sight.
You have come to Zion, city of living Divine, heavenly Jerusalem.
You have come to festival assembly, enrolled firstborn, Judge of all, and completed just ones.
You have come to Jesus, new covenant mediator, and better-speaking blood/life.
Do not refuse the One speaking.
The voice that shook earth promises another shaking.
What can be shaken is removed so the unshaken remains.
Receiving unshakable reign, serve with grace, reverence, and awe.
Our Divine is consuming fire.

contemplative_note: Hebrews 12 is endurance in the cloud: discipline becomes healing, Zion opens as festival, and grace receives an unshakable reign.
